properties.name: Eothraim
properties.description_text: The territory of the Eothraim comprises the vast, fertile southern plains and rolling grasslands of southern Dor Rhunen, southwest of the Eorstan Hills and extending west toward Greenwood the Great. Watered by the Warwater and Surubeki rivers, this lush environment is home to the Eothraim—fierce, honest, and courageous horse-masters who live as semi-nomadic herders and hunters.
properties.description_summary: Great tall-grass plains where the Warwater and Surubeki feed the pastures of a semi-nomadic horse-people. The wind rules the landscape and the horizon is always wide. The Eothraim live by following their herds.

properties.name: Gramuz
properties.description_text: Gramuz is a rugged northern wilderland region of Dor Rhunen, situated north of Dorwinion and south of the Iron Hills. It is characterized by undulating lowlands, rolling hills, and scattered mixed woodlands. It is a harsh frontier territory populated by semi-nomadic Easterling clans and hardy Northmen who struggle for survival against the bitter cold winds blowing from the northeast Sea of Rhûn.
properties.description_summary: Land of low hills and sparse woodland between Dorwinion and the Iron Hills. Cold northeast winds and semi-nomadic Easterling clans who survive by hunting, fishing and trading furs. A harsh frontier territory.

properties.name: Mirkwood Wilds
properties.description_text: Southern Mirkwood and Southern Rhovanion form a wild, embattled region stretching from the Old Forest Road down to the Dagorlad and the Emyn Muil, dominated by the dark forest around Dol Guldur, the horse-plains of Talath Harroch, and the Anduin vales and Brown Lands that lie between Mirkwood and Gondor. Geology consists of primarily sedimentary strata of limestone and shale, with significant granite projections such as the cinder cone of amon lanc and the mountains of mirkwood.
properties.description_summary: Southern Mirkwood dominated by the shadow of Dol Guldur. Dark trees with interlocking branches, ground carpeted in roots and fungi, and the volcanic cone of Amon Lanc presiding over a poisoned land where few good creatures survive.

properties.name: Woodland Realm
properties.description_text: Northern Mirkwood is the northern half of the great forest once known as Greenwood the Great, stretching between the Anduin, the Grey Mountains, the Withered Heath and the upper River Running, and including Thranduil’s woodland realm, the Long Lake, the Lonely Mountain and the Iron Hills.
properties.description_summary: Northern Mirkwood governed by Thranduil from his underground halls. Deep forest of oaks and firs, Silvan Elves patrolling rune-marked paths, and the River Running as the trade artery eastward.

properties.name: Parth Celebrant
properties.description_text: Parth Celebrant, the Field of Celebrant, is a wide, rolling grassy plain nestled between the Great River Anduin and the Limlight. It is famous as the historical battleground where Eorl the Young led the Éothéod to save the armies of Gondor, leading to the gift of Rohan.
properties.description_summary: Green plain between the Anduin and the Limlight, famous as the battlefield where Eorl saved Gondor. Tall grass, willows on the banks and a silence broken only by the wind and the memory of the hooves that once galloped here.

properties.name: Dor Rhunen
properties.description_text: Dor Rhunen, the 'Land of the East,' comprises the semi-arid, vast southern plains and rolling grasslands surrounding the southern shores of the Sea of Rhûn. Extending southwest toward the desolate Battle Plain of Dagorlad and bordered to the south by the ash-choked fences of Mordor, this is a harsh, wind-swept buffer territory. It is heavily influenced by the cultures of Rohan and the Wold to the west, but dominated by exceptionally brutal nomadic Easterling tribes, such as the Variags, Nuriags, and Asdriags, who frequently raid the trade caravans.
properties.description_summary: Vast semi-arid steppes south of the Sea of Rhûn, swept by Easterling nomadic tribes of brutal reputation. The Dagorlad closes the south and the shadow of Mordor haunts the southwest horizon. A land without law or mercy.

properties.name: Anorien
properties.description_text: Anórien is the northern fief of Gondor that occupies the strip of land between the northern slopes and valleys of the White Mountains and the west bank of the Anduin, opposite northern Ithilien. Established towards the end of the Second Age along with Ithilien and Lebennin as part of Gondor’s Anduin heartland, it later remains the only Gondorian fief north of the White Mountains when Calenardhon is ceded to the Éothéod to become Rohan. The region is defined by mountain-fed valleys, tall grasslands and scattered woods, pierced by the Great West Road and guarded by beacon-peaks and the Drúadan Forest.
properties.description_summary: Northern fief of Gondor between the White Mountains and the Anduin, with mountain-fed valleys, tall grasslands and the Great West Road. The beacon-peaks that signal war frame a land of passage guarded between two great powers.

properties.name: Dagorlad
properties.description_text: Dagorlad is the wide, flat battle-plain north and northeast of Udûn and the main pass into Mordor, once covered with wild grains and grasses and now a bare, declining expanse dotted with cairns and barrows where the Last Alliance fought Sauron at the end of the Second Age.
properties.description_summary: The great battle-plain before Mordor, bare and ash-grey since the Last Alliance. Barrows of the fallen, black mud that smells of ancient metal and the Dead Marshes to the northwest with their flickering lights. A land of ill omen.

properties.name: Druadan Forest
properties.description_text: The Drúadan Forest (S: 'Tawar-in-Drúedain') is an ancient, dense, and secretive oakwood situated in northeastern Gondor along the northern slopes of the White Mountains. Bordered by the beacon-hills of Eilenach and Nardol, this forest is the ancestral home of the Woses (Drúedain), a primeval, secretive race of master woodsmen who lead primitive, clan-based lives in hidden hollows and valleys completely apart from Gondorian civilization.
properties.description_summary: Ancient oakwood on the northern flanks of the White Mountains, ancestral home of the secretive Woses. Gnarled trees with hanging mosses, grotesque stone carvings on the hidden paths, and the Drúedain invisible as shadows among the roots.

properties.name: Esgaroth
properties.description_text: Northern Mirkwood is the northern half of the great forest once known as Greenwood the Great, stretching between the Anduin, the Grey Mountains, the Withered Heath and the upper River Running, and including Thranduil’s woodland realm, the Long Lake, the Lonely Mountain and the Iron Hills.
properties.description_summary: Trading town on piles over the Long Lake, beneath the shadow of Erebor. Canals for streets, creaking walkways, and Dorwinion wine and Dwarven jewels as currency. A wealthy, practical enclave of northern merchants.

properties.name: Lebennin
properties.description_text: Lebennin, the Place of Five Waters, is the rich, river-laced heartland of Gondor between the White Mountains and the Anduin, a fertile province of rolling fields, busy ports and Sea-lords whose fleets fight Corsairs and smugglers across the Bay of Belfalas.
properties.description_summary: Agricultural heartland of southern Gondor, laced by five rivers that fertilise its plains and wetlands. Grey-stone villages, busy river-ports and the densest population in all Gondor make this a prosperous and vital land.

properties.name: Harondor
properties.description_text: Harondor is the arid, contested plateau between the Poros river and the Vale of Harnen, bounded by the Ephel Dûath to the east and the Belfalas coast to the west; a perpetually disputed borderland between Gondor and the Haruze/Umbarean powers, drier and hotter than the Gondorian heartland. Geology consists of igneous bedrock, with basalt common near the coast and granite further inland. valuable coastal salt panning is a major industry.
properties.description_summary: Arid plateau between the Poros and Harnen rivers, perpetually disputed between Gondor and Umbar. Basalt and granite, poor soil, coastal salt flats and ruins of both civilisations layered over one another in a land of volatile loyalties.

properties.name: Anfalas
properties.description_text: Southern Gondor comprises the fertile river valleys, coastal plains, highland vales and mountain flanks between the White Mountains (Ered Nimrais) and the Bay of Belfalas, encompassing the provinces of Lebennin, Belfalas/Dor-en-Ernil, Lamedon, Anfalas and the Pinnath Gelin—the heartland of Númenórean civilization in Middle-earth.
properties.description_summary: Long shore of salt marshes, estuaries and reed-beds where sea and land blur. Fishermen in flat-bottomed boats and sheep-farming on the Pinnath Gelin sustain this region quietly forgotten by politics but essential to the south.

properties.name: Westmarch
properties.description_text: Rohan is a beautiful territory and one of the jewels of the Gondorian crown, a wide land of rolling pastures and coombes dominated by the towering peaks of the White Mountains, home of the semi‑nomadic Horse‑lords who became the Rohirrim after T.A. 2510.
properties.description_summary: Western frontier of Gondor between the Fords of Isen and the Andrast. Heather-covered hills, swift rivers and a Gondorian presence that fades westward. Dunlendish farmers have filled the void left by the departed Dúnedain.

properties.name: South Arthedain
properties.description_text: Arthedain is the northern Dúnedain kingdom in Eriador, cradled between the Blue Mountains and the Misty Mountains and bounded by the rivers Lhûn and Baranduin, a cool, sparsely populated realm of rocky hills, downs, marshes, and the Shire, ruled from Fornost as the last Númenórean realm in the North in T.A. 1640.
properties.description_summary: The most gentle and inhabited part of Arthedain, with rolling hills, green hedgerows and the Shire of the Hobbits to the south. The Brandywine to the east and the Great East Road connect this fertile land to the wider world.

properties.name: Minhiriath
properties.description_text: Cardolan, the Land of Red Hills, is the southern successor-kingdom of Arnor between the Baranduin and the Gwathló–Mitheithel, a windswept land of treeless plains, red downs, marshy river basins and the barrow‑haunted hills whose shattered Dúnedain realm never fully recovered from war, civil strife and plague.
properties.description_summary: Deforested plain between the Baranduin and the Gwathló, once dense woodland felled by the Númenóreans for their shipyards. Depopulated since the Great Plague, with abandoned villages in the scrub and Dúnedain barrows across the open land.

properties.name: Rast Vorn
properties.description_text: Cardolan, the Land of Red Hills, is the southern successor-kingdom of Arnor between the Baranduin and the Gwathló–Mitheithel, a windswept land of treeless plains, red downs, marshy river basins and the barrow‑haunted hills whose shattered Dúnedain realm never fully recovered from war, civil strife and plague.
properties.description_summary: Cape of black rock cliffs jutting into Belegaer south of the Gwathló, with an interior of old twisted oaks sheltering primitive Gwathuirim tribes. Inhospitable and forgotten, it serves as refuge for those who flee from everything.

properties.name: Harithilien
properties.description_text: Harithilien is South Ithilien, a distinct, fertile, and beautiful region bordering Harondor and Mordor. It is characterized by volcanic soils enriched by the Ephel Dúath, featuring ancient ruined estates of Gondor, hidden ranger outposts, and lush vegetation.
properties.description_summary: South Ithilien, fertile and aromatic thanks to the volcanic soils of the Ephel Dûath. Olive groves, vineyards and ruined Gondorian villas amid overflowing vegetation, watched over by Rangers hidden in grottos and rock crevices.

properties.name: Lamedon
properties.description_text: Southern Gondor comprises the fertile river valleys, coastal plains, highland vales and mountain flanks between the White Mountains (Ered Nimrais) and the Bay of Belfalas, encompassing the provinces of Lebennin, Belfalas/Dor-en-Ernil, Lamedon, Anfalas and the Pinnath Gelin—the heartland of Númenórean civilization in Middle-earth.
properties.description_summary: Mountain province in the upper valleys of the White Mountains, rich in iron, copper and silver. Slate-roofed villages clinging to hillsides, stout clan-loyal miners and mountain passes that snow closes for months at a time.

properties.name: Dor-en-Emyl
properties.description_text: Dor‑en‑Ernil, the Land of the Prince, is a princely fief in south‑central Gondor encompassing Belfalas and neighboring lowlands and highlands; ruled from the coastal city of Dol Amroth, it is famed for its White‑ships, Elven‑tinged nobility, and constant tension with Umbar and the Eredrim highlanders.
properties.description_summary: Southern Gondor's princely peninsula governed from Dol Amroth, city of white towers above the sea. Silver-rich mountains, rugged cliffs, Elvish-blooded nobility and the sacred kirinki birds make this the most singular land in Gondor.

properties.name: Enedhwaith
properties.description_text: Dunland is the rugged foothill and plateau country south of the Glanduin, east of the Gwathló and north of the Isen, a land of highlands and plains ruled by fractious Dunlending clans who herd, raid and scheme along the vital Old South Road and the Isen Gap.
properties.description_summary: Wild land between the Gwathló and the Isen, of heathland, peat bogs and low oaks. Depopulated since the wars of the north, the Gwathuirim tribes survive at its margins. A landscape of bleak beauty under vast Atlantic skies.

properties.name: Tol Fuin
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Dark isle off the northern coast of Forlindon, remnant of the highlands of Beleriand drowned beneath the waves. Black vertical cliffs, cold peat bogs of perpetual mist and the melancholy echo of a land lost under Belegaer.

properties.name: Tolfalas
properties.description_text: Lebennin, the Place of Five Waters, is the rich, river-laced heartland of Gondor between the White Mountains and the Anduin, a fertile province of rolling fields, busy ports and Sea-lords whose fleets fight Corsairs and smugglers across the Bay of Belfalas.
properties.description_summary: Volcanic island in the Bay of Belfalas with peaks, fumaroles and hot springs. The sacred oiolair trees grow only here; sea turtles nest on its dark beaches. Dramatic beauty and geological instability in equal measure.

properties.name: Iron Hills
properties.description_text: The Grey Mountains (Ered Mithrin) form a long, cold northern range between Mirkwood and the Forodwaith, with abrupt cliffs above the Wilderland, gentler northern slopes fading into tundra and the Talath Oiohelka, and the dragon-haunted basin of the Withered Heath between the eastern forks of the range.
properties.description_summary: System of low hills east of Mirkwood, worked by Dwarves for iron, coal and silver. Gallery mouths in the hillsides, smelter slag heaps and beneath it all a Dwarven city of workshops and subterranean markets of hard steel.

properties.name: Dale
properties.description_text: The Grey Mountains (Ered Mithrin) form a long, cold northern range between Mirkwood and the Forodwaith, with abrupt cliffs above the Wilderland, gentler northern slopes fading into tundra and the Talath Oiohelka, and the dragon-haunted basin of the Withered Heath between the eastern forks of the range.
properties.description_summary: Market town at the feet of Erebor, rebuilt after the death of Smaug. Cobbled terraced streets, the craft of toys and bells, and markets where the Dwarven world, the lake-men and southern traders all converge.

properties.name: Ronalindon
properties.description_text: Ronalindon is a serene coastal subregion of Harlindon situated along the shores of the Gulf of Lhûn and Belegaer. Famed for its peaceful forest glades, quiet river estuaries, and coastal mudflats, it represents a pristine maritime-woodland environment where Elves live in harmony with both the forest and the sea. Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Southern coast of the Gulf of Lhûn where oak and ash forests reach down to the water. Labyrinthine tidal estuaries, otters among the roots and Elves who build light boats to sail the calm gulf.

properties.name: Ered Luin
properties.description_text: Ered Luin, also known as the Blue Mountains, is an ancient mountain range separating Lindon from Eriador. Extending 590 miles from the Ice Bay of Forochel down to Orod Elu in the south, the range is formed of grey rock with a bluish tint caused by a high concentration of copper ores. The mountains are riddled with natural caverns, marble and limestone formations, and once held precious metals, though Dwarvish mining has left them depleted of gold except for minor remote veins. Geology consists of marble and limestone with folded sandstone and shale foothills. abundant copper, iron, and tin ores are actively mined by the dwarves (naugrim).
properties.description_summary: 590-mile range of grey copper-tinged rock that appears burnished indigo in raking light. Ancient Dwarven cities lost in its depths, and Thorin's folk still mining the eastern side while cold torrents feed Lindon's rivers to the west.

properties.name: Ardolyalinya
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Inner northern hills of Harlindon where old oaks and birches reach gold in autumn and the Sindar Elves live in quiet contemplation. The sea is heard but not seen here, lending this land its gentle melancholy.

properties.name: Belegaer sea
properties.description_text: The Belegaer, or Great Sea, is the vast ocean separating Middle-earth (Endor) from the Undying Lands of Aman in the far West. In the coastal domains of Lindon, Arnor, and Gondor, it comprises key areas such as the Gulf of Lhûn (Nen Lhûn) and the Bay of Belfalas (Nen Belfalas). The deep gulf of Nen Lhûn, formed in the cataclysms of the First Age, serves as the calm and misty harbor for the Elven ships sailing from Mithlond, rich in salmon, lobsters, and shellfish. Further south, the vast Bay of Belfalas rests on Endor's continental shelf, driven by the warm, counterclockwise Southron Stream which collides with the cold Great Stream off the Cape of Andrast, creating turbulent, dangerous waters home to sharks, grey sperm whales, and giant squids.
properties.description_summary: The Great Sea separating Middle-earth from Aman. The misty Gulf of Lhûn in the north for Elven ships; the warm stormy Bay of Belfalas in the south. Whales in its depths and the irresistible call of the West.

properties.name: Lairiardhon
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Gentle hills in the heart of Harlindon, known for the Elvish tradition of singing at dawn from hilltops facing the sea. Ancient oaks in open meadows and river-pebble paths between the mountains and the coast.

properties.name: Lildardhon
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Open plain of Harlindon swept by the gulf wind. Grass that bends in silver waves, heather in spring bloom and Elvish bonfires dotting the dark on gathering nights. Bright and open with a gentle underlying melancholy.

properties.name: South Harlindon
properties.description_text: South Harlindon is a green, quiet coastal and forest region south of the Gulf of Lhûn. Historically a fief under Celeborn, Harlindon is characterized by lowlands cloaked in rich deciduous and coniferous forests and a long-term peaceful rural atmosphere. The eastern foothills are used by Dwarves for active coal and iron mines. Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Southernmost fringe of Lindon, where oak and conifer forests reach a rocky shoreline of tidal inlets. Dwarven mining roads in the eastern foothills and a small Sindar Elvish community living quietly by the sea.

properties.name: Ardquetto
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Narrow valleys in Harlindon's northern foothills where short rivers run down from the Ered Luin to the Gulf. Alders and willows along salmon-rich streams; a place tied by tradition to the Nandor women who first settled Lindon.

properties.name: Ered Luin Vales
properties.description_text: The Ered Luin Vales encompass the central valleys, river passes, and sub-alpine basins of the Blue Mountains. This region is centered around the Nan-i-Naugrim (Dwarven Vale) and the headwaters of the River Annuduin, where the Dwarves established a complex network of fourteen holds, including Merlost, built on the ruins of ancient Belegost.
properties.description_summary: Sub-alpine valleys and gorges of the Ered Luin with fourteen Dwarven hall-fortresses, including Merlost over the ruins of Belegost. Firs on rock walls, snowmelt waterfalls and the constant ring of Dwarven picks.

properties.name: Central Anduin
properties.description_text: The High Pass (Cirith Forn en Andrath, S. 'High-climbing Pass of the North') cuts through the central Misty Mountains connecting Rivendell and Eriador in the west to the upper Anduin Vales and Rhovanion in the east. It encompasses the Orcish stronghold of Goblin-gate, the eyrie of the Great Eagles, the Northman market-town of Maethelburg, the western foothills of Rhudaur, and the broad river plain of the Anduin. Geology consists of limestone and granite with massive igneous basalt intrusions. western foothills consist of red sandstone, while eastern valleys contain oily black shale; rich in copper, iron, silver, gold, and mithril.
properties.description_summary: The High Pass cutting through the central Misty Mountains between Rivendell and Rhovanion. The Goblin-gate, the Great Eagles on the peaks and the market of Maethelburg define this vital and dangerous crossing.

properties.name: Ronen-in-Anduin
properties.description_text: Ronen-in-Anduin is the eastern floodplains and low rolling hills bordering the Great River Anduin as it passes through the territory of Rhovanion. It represents a transition zone between the river valleys and the wide eastern steppes.
properties.description_summary: Eastern floodplains of the Anduin in Rhovanion, transitional land between the river valleys and the steppes. Fertile silt banks, Northmen smallholdings and the wide slow river with its willow islands.

properties.name: Woodmen
properties.description_text: Southern Mirkwood and Southern Rhovanion form a wild, embattled region stretching from the Old Forest Road down to the Dagorlad and the Emyn Muil, dominated by the dark forest around Dol Guldur, the horse-plains of Talath Harroch, and the Anduin vales and Brown Lands that lie between Mirkwood and Gondor. Geology consists of primarily sedimentary strata of limestone and shale, with significant granite projections such as the cinder cone of amon lanc and the mountains of mirkwood.
properties.description_summary: Communities of woodcutters and hunters on the western fringe of southern Mirkwood, between the Anduin and the darkness of the forest. A life of constant negotiation with Wargs, Dol Guldur's agents and the protective Elvish bows to the north.

properties.name: Nan Anduin
properties.description_text: Nan Anduin is the long, wide, and highly fertile alluvial valley through which the Great River Anduin flows between the Misty Mountains and Mirkwood. It contains some of the richest soil in Middle-earth, supporting scattered communities of Beornings, Woodmen, and Northman pioneers.
properties.description_summary: Alluvial valley between the Misty Mountains and Mirkwood, with the richest soil in the north. The Beornings levy tolls at the fords, their giant hives scent the air, and the river fills the valley with its constant murmur.

properties.name: Central Misty Mountains
properties.description_text: The High Pass (Cirith Forn en Andrath, S. 'High-climbing Pass of the North') cuts through the central Misty Mountains connecting Rivendell and Eriador in the west to the upper Anduin Vales and Rhovanion in the east. It encompasses the Orcish stronghold of Goblin-gate, the eyrie of the Great Eagles, the Northman market-town of Maethelburg, the western foothills of Rhudaur, and the broad river plain of the Anduin. Geology consists of limestone and granite with massive igneous basalt intrusions. western foothills consist of red sandstone, while eastern valleys contain oily black shale; rich in copper, iron, silver, gold, and mithril.
properties.description_summary: The stony heart of the Misty Mountains with the High Pass as the only crossing. Permanently snow-capped granite peaks, the Goblin-gate hidden in red sandstone slopes and passes that kill through blizzard and avalanche.

properties.name: Tower Hills
properties.description_text: The Tower Hills, or Emyn Beraid, are a range of green, rolling hills located west of the Shire. On their highest crests stand three ancient Elven-towers built by Gil-galad, where the Elendil Stone (palantír) was guarded for millennia.
properties.description_summary: Line of limestone hills with three ancient Elvish towers on their crests, the tallest housing the palantír that gazes only westward. Short turf, heather and hawthorn in spring; a near-sacred quiet between the Shire and the sea.

properties.name: West Rhudaur
properties.description_text: Rhudaur is the eastern successor state of Arnor, a rugged, sparsely settled land between the Ettenmoors, the Trollshaws and the Angle. It stretches from the Ettenmoors and Ettendales in the north down through the Pinefells and Trollshaws to the rolling lowlands between the rivers Mitheithel and Bruinen, forming a harsh frontier of jagged hills, evergreen forests, boggy vales and predator-haunted woodlands.
properties.description_summary: Western Rhudaur, where dry-stone farms and working mills survive as remnants of Dúnedain prosperity. Now held by Hillmen allied to the Witch-king; black-pine hills, cold torrents and hollows where darkness falls quickly.

properties.name: En Egladil
properties.description_text: Rhudaur is the eastern successor state of Arnor, a rugged, sparsely settled land between the Ettenmoors, the Trollshaws and the Angle. It stretches from the Ettenmoors and Ettendales in the north down through the Pinefells and Trollshaws to the rolling lowlands between the rivers Mitheithel and Bruinen, forming a harsh frontier of jagged hills, evergreen forests, boggy vales and predator-haunted woodlands.
properties.description_summary: The Angle enclosed between the Mitheithel and Bruinen, once the heart of Dúnedain Rhudaur. Deep fertile soil now largely abandoned, palisaded Hillmen villages on the hilltops and Dúnedain tower-ruins rising among the trees.

properties.name: Trollshaws
properties.description_text: Rhudaur is the eastern successor state of Arnor, a rugged, sparsely settled land between the Ettenmoors, the Trollshaws and the Angle. It stretches from the Ettenmoors and Ettendales in the north down through the Pinefells and Trollshaws to the rolling lowlands between the rivers Mitheithel and Bruinen, forming a harsh frontier of jagged hills, evergreen forests, boggy vales and predator-haunted woodlands.
properties.description_summary: Labyrinth of shadowed valleys and twisted oaks south of the Mitheithel, where Stone-trolls keep their caves in limestone hillsides. Dim light, deer-tracks to nowhere and bone-piles at cave entrances mark this dangerous stretch of country.

properties.name: Ettenmoors
properties.description_text: Rhudaur is the eastern successor state of Arnor, a rugged, sparsely settled land between the Ettenmoors, the Trollshaws and the Angle. It stretches from the Ettenmoors and Ettendales in the north down through the Pinefells and Trollshaws to the rolling lowlands between the rivers Mitheithel and Bruinen, forming a harsh frontier of jagged hills, evergreen forests, boggy vales and predator-haunted woodlands.
properties.description_summary: Northern moorlands of Rhudaur, windy peat-bogs and heather hills claimed by Trolls and Orcs since the fall of the Dúnedain. Brown water, grey horizons and the snow-capped Misty Mountains visible only on rare clear days.

properties.name: Coldfells
properties.description_text: The High Pass (Cirith Forn en Andrath, S. 'High-climbing Pass of the North') cuts through the central Misty Mountains connecting Rivendell and Eriador in the west to the upper Anduin Vales and Rhovanion in the east. It encompasses the Orcish stronghold of Goblin-gate, the eyrie of the Great Eagles, the Northman market-town of Maethelburg, the western foothills of Rhudaur, and the broad river plain of the Anduin. Geology consists of limestone and granite with massive igneous basalt intrusions. western foothills consist of red sandstone, while eastern valleys contain oily black shale; rich in copper, iron, silver, gold, and mithril.
properties.description_summary: Northern foothills of the Misty Mountains where bare rock and north wind admit neither comfort nor shelter. Mountain Orcs, wolves and Eagles are the chief inhabitants; the trails to the High Pass begin among this bare stone.

properties.name: Angmar
properties.description_text: Angmar is a cold, militarized realm north of Arnor, ruled by the Witch-king of the Nazgûl; it exists primarily as a war machine, built on harsh land, hardened frontier peasants, and armies of men, orcs, and monsters. Geology consists of deeply eroded limestone that robs the surface soil of water, with igneous and metamorphic outcrops near the mountains. carn dûm is built on a massive block of dull red granite.
properties.description_summary: The Witch-king's war realm of grey walls, muddy camps and enforced misery. Carn Dûm sits on dark red granite among mountains that cage everyone within. Cold, Orcs and fear are the three pillars of Angmar.

properties.name: Ered Luin
properties.description_text: Ered Luin, also known as the Blue Mountains, is an ancient mountain range separating Lindon from Eriador. Extending 590 miles from the Ice Bay of Forochel down to Orod Elu in the south, the range is formed of grey rock with a bluish tint caused by a high concentration of copper ores. The mountains are riddled with natural caverns, marble and limestone formations, and once held precious metals, though Dwarvish mining has left them depleted of gold except for minor remote veins. Geology consists of marble and limestone with folded sandstone and shale foothills. abundant copper, iron, and tin ores are actively mined by the dwarves (naugrim).
properties.description_summary: Northernmost and most abrupt section of the Ered Luin, with a greenish-black slate tinge and passes held in snow nine months of the year. Dwarven galleries to the east; snowmelt rapids feed the rivers of Forlindon to the west.

properties.name: Numeriador
properties.description_text: Numeriador is the cold, rugged, and wind-swept northern plateau of Eriador, lying north of the Hills of Evendim. It is a sparsely populated, rolling wilderness of grasses and stunted shrubs, once part of the kingdom of Arnor.
properties.description_summary: Cold, wind-swept plateau north of the Hills of Evendim, the edge of the inhabited world before Forochel. Thin soil over rock, juniper scrub, scarce shepherds and wolves that grow bolder every winter.

properties.name: Ascahiriand
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Northernmost sub-region of Forlindon, the most rain-swept and mist-prone part of Lindon. Moss-bogs, heath and silver birches in sheltered valleys; rounded hills of intense green grass and a watchful silence facing the wild north.

properties.name: En Udanoriath
properties.description_text: En Udanoriath, meaning the 'No-Man's-Land' or the 'Dwelling-less Waste', is a bleak, barren, and wind-scarred frontier zone located between the southern border of Angmar and the northern boundaries of Arthedain (near the North Downs). This cold highland waste is a desolate, rocky steppe plagued by bitter northern winds and constant military skirmishes. It serves as a buffer and raiding corridor where the Witch-king's patrols clash with Arnor's remnants, overseen by spy-towers like Mindil Cargash.
properties.description_summary: Barren steppe buffer zone between Angmar and the North Downs of Arthedain, the Witch-king's raiding corridor. Rocky and wind-scoured, with spy-towers disguised as ruins; in winter the blizzard kills in the open within hours.

properties.name: Mänoriand
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Inner Forlindon along the Linduin, a land of constant drizzle and moss-green on every surface. Damp heather hills, pool-fed waterfalls in side valleys and Elvish homes built to face the sound of water. The quiet, rainy heart of Lindon.

properties.name: Celanoriand
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: The most storm-exposed coast of Forlindon, ironically named the Land of the Bright Sun. Thirty-metre cliff-waves in winter, tree-bending westerlies, but in calm the sea turns to living silver and the clifftops to emerald. A land of extremes.

properties.name: Sandariand
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: The most settled sub-region of Forlindon, mild and green thanks to the Gulf of Lhûn. Oak forests and clover meadows, the oldest and best-preserved Elvish settlements in northern Lindon and the road to the port of Forlond.

properties.name: Talath Muil
properties.description_text: Talath Muil ('Drear Plain') is the rugged highland plain forming the northwestern marches of Arthedain, dividing northern Arthedain from the Wash Tundra and the Vale of the Lhûn. It is a windswept, largely treeless upland of low grass and open grassland, dotted with large wildflower meadows and small copses of ash and birch. To the east it abuts the rocky western edges of the northern Hills of Evendim, whose rough ground is difficult to traverse even on foot, and whose rocky hills gradually mellow into grass-covered downs and finally spill northeast into the dry plateau of the Rammas Forod.
properties.description_summary: High windswept plateau on the northwest marches of Arthedain, with short grass, wildflower meadows and ash groves bent eastward by the constant west wind. Wide and silent; the Ered Luin visible on clear days to the south.

properties.name: Rammas Formen
properties.description_text: The Rammas Forod (Sindarin 'Great Wall of the North'; Adûnaic Ramênytunêny, actually 'South-wall') is a low chain of hills dividing the down-lands of northern Arthedain from the watershed of the Wash Tundra. To the west it widens into the Talath Muil, while to the east its hills thin out and merge into the Everlasting Plain. Born as debris from the collapse of the Iron Mountains in the War of Wrath, the ridge has shed rock and soil into the valleys on either side, allowing modest forests to grow along its flanks. Clear air and narrow defiles offer room for small farms and gardens, and for a time the Rammas served as a religious retreat for the Faithful of Arnor.
properties.description_summary: Natural limestone escarpment crossing northern Arthedain, the kingdom's first geological line of defence. Blackthorn and hawthorn on its slopes, few watched passes and the sensation of crossing a threshold into colder country.

properties.name: Evendim Hills
properties.description_text: The Evendim Hills (Sindarin Emyn Uial, 'Hills of Twilight') are the upland region around the valley of the Cornalas and the northern shore of Lake Evendim (Nenuial) in western Arthedain. For one season they are pale brown under constant cool mists cupped in the valley, for another they lie soft and white under snow, and in the Awakening and the Poem the valley becomes a shifting tapestry of lush greens. Though unproductive by southern standards—steep hillsides strain shepherds’ knees and narrow vales have thin soil—the hills have long been the heart of Arthedain, their rippling waters and mists inspiring poets and musicians.
properties.description_summary: Hills encircling Lake Nenuial in western Arthedain, with the marble ruins of Annúminas on the southern shore. Silver mist on full-moon nights wrapping the ruins in ghostly light; great beauty and greater melancholy.

properties.name: Fornost
properties.description_text: Fornost Erain, the 'Norbury of the Kings', was once the grand, fortified northern capital of the kingdom of Arthedain, located at the northern end of the North Downs. Now a silent, massive ruin known as Deadmen's Dike, it is haunted by ghosts, wraiths, and the memories of the fallen Dúnedain. The ruins are characterized by crumbling stone ramparts, collapsed halls, and overgrown ditches.
properties.description_summary: Fortified capital of Arthedain on the North Downs, still the heart of the last Dúnedain kingdom of the north. Grey walls, maintained ramparts, the Royal Court and the Seers; but more empty houses with each generation and Angmar darkening the north.

properties.name: Old Forest
properties.description_text: The Old Forest is an ancient, sentient, and deeply shadowed woodland bordering the eastern Shire. Known for its hostile, moving trees and gnarled roots, it is ruled by the mysterious Old Man Willow and protected by Tom Bombadil.
properties.description_summary: Ancient and consciously hostile woodland on the eastern edge of the Shire, with paths that shift to disorient and Old Man Willow drowsing travellers from the dark water. Only Tom Bombadil's voice keeps it calm.

properties.name: South Downs
properties.description_text: Arthedain is the northern Dúnedain kingdom in Eriador, cradled between the Blue Mountains and the Misty Mountains and bounded by the rivers Lhûn and Baranduin, a cool, sparsely populated realm of rocky hills, downs, marshes, and the Shire, ruled from Fornost as the last Númenórean realm in the North in T.A. 1640.
properties.description_summary: Chalk hills of southern Cardolan, once the granary of the southern Arnor kingdom. Dúnedain towers on the crests, wild pasture where wheat fields once grew and nomadic shepherds in a land that history has forgotten.

properties.name: Mintyrnath
properties.description_text: Cardolan, the Land of Red Hills, is the southern successor-kingdom of Arnor between the Baranduin and the Gwathló–Mitheithel, a windswept land of treeless plains, red downs, marshy river basins and the barrow‑haunted hills whose shattered Dúnedain realm never fully recovered from war, civil strife and plague.
properties.description_summary: Marshy coastal strip where the Baranduin and Gwathló meet Belegaer in a labyrinth of marshes and shifting sandbars. Migratory birds in their thousands in autumn, tidal fords only the Gwathuirim know and land that changes with every tide.

properties.name: Weather Hills
properties.description_text: Arthedain is the northern Dúnedain kingdom in Eriador, cradled between the Blue Mountains and the Misty Mountains and bounded by the rivers Lhûn and Baranduin, a cool, sparsely populated realm of rocky hills, downs, marshes, and the Shire, ruled from Fornost as the last Númenórean realm in the North in T.A. 1640.
properties.description_summary: Ridge of rough limestone on Arthedain's eastern border, crowned by Amon Sûl where the chief northern palantír once stood. Dry east wind stripping the crests bare and views across all Eriador on clear days.

properties.name: Rohan
properties.description_text: Rohan is a beautiful territory and one of the jewels of the Gondorian crown, a wide land of rolling pastures and coombes dominated by the towering peaks of the White Mountains, home of the semi‑nomadic Horse‑lords who became the Rohirrim after T.A. 2510.
properties.description_summary: Vast grassland plain between the White Mountains and Fangorn, ruled by the Rohirrim and their great horse-herds. The wind always blows, Edoras shines on its green hill and life here is perpetual motion.

properties.name: Wold
properties.description_text: Rohan is a beautiful territory and one of the jewels of the Gondorian crown, a wide land of rolling pastures and coombes dominated by the towering peaks of the White Mountains, home of the semi‑nomadic Horse‑lords who became the Rohirrim after T.A. 2510.
properties.description_summary: Northeastern Rohan, transitional between the horse-plains and the edge of southern Mirkwood. Taller, darker grass, the Entwash's peat-yellow waters and the most guarded and wary Rohirrim of the kingdom.

properties.name: Apariach
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Northern promontory of Forlindon pointing into Belegaer. Dark fractured cliffs battered by the sea, tall heather and peat bog inland, and seabird colonies on every ledge. On clear days Tol Fuin is visible to the northwest.

properties.name: Vinyalas
properties.description_text: Lindon is the westernmost Elvish realm of Middle-earth, the remnant of Ossiriand that survived the War of Wrath. After the breaking and flooding of Beleriand, the Great Sea breached the Blue Mountains to form the Gulf of Lhûn, dividing Lindon into Forlindon (North Lindon) and Harlindon (South Lindon), with the River Lhûn as the eastern border against Arnor. Throughout the Second and early Third Age it is the principal Elven coastal realm and home of the Grey Havens (Mithlond). Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.
properties.description_summary: Southern Forlindon along the lower Linduin, the last sub-region to be named by the Elves. Dense alders, spring-flooded meadows with frogs and storks and the wide slow green river that speaks only of time, not history.

properties.name: Forovirian
properties.description_text: Mount Gundabad is the northernmost and one of the highest peaks of the Misty Mountains, a triple‑spired volcanic giant honeycombed into a vast Orc‑city and forge complex, capital of the Orcs of the Mountains and a key client realm of Angmar.
properties.description_summary: Gundabad, the triple-spired volcanic capital of the Mountain Orcs, with hundreds of underground levels of forges and armories. Black rock, dirty snow and smelter smoke rising from chimneys cut into the living stone.

properties.name: Talath Uichel
properties.description_text: Talath Uichel, the Plain of Everlasting Cold, is the vast tundra interior east of Forochel, structured into green belts and herding grounds, while beyond its northern edge lies the Landless Land, the polar ice-cap desert at the very end of Middle-earth.
properties.description_summary: Polar tundra east of Forochel, with impossibly brief summers of flowers and caribou herds and endless brutal winters under metres of snow. Beyond its northern edge lies the Landless Land, where the known world ends.

properties.name: North Misty Mountains
properties.description_text: The High Pass (Cirith Forn en Andrath, S. 'High-climbing Pass of the North') cuts through the central Misty Mountains connecting Rivendell and Eriador in the west to the upper Anduin Vales and Rhovanion in the east. It encompasses the Orcish stronghold of Goblin-gate, the eyrie of the Great Eagles, the Northman market-town of Maethelburg, the western foothills of Rhudaur, and the broad river plain of the Anduin. Geology consists of limestone and granite with massive igneous basalt intrusions. western foothills consist of red sandstone, while eastern valleys contain oily black shale; rich in copper, iron, silver, gold, and mithril.
properties.description_summary: Wildest section of the Misty Mountains, between Gundabad and the Mountains of Iron. Red sandstone slopes with fir valleys to the west, oily black shale and coal seams to the east. The Great Eagles nest above and Gundabad's shadow covers all.

properties.name: Wilderness of Arnor
properties.description_text: The High Pass (Cirith Forn en Andrath, S. 'High-climbing Pass of the North') cuts through the central Misty Mountains connecting Rivendell and Eriador in the west to the upper Anduin Vales and Rhovanion in the east. It encompasses the Orcish stronghold of Goblin-gate, the eyrie of the Great Eagles, the Northman market-town of Maethelburg, the western foothills of Rhudaur, and the broad river plain of the Anduin. Geology consists of limestone and granite with massive igneous basalt intrusions. western foothills consist of red sandstone, while eastern valleys contain oily black shale; rich in copper, iron, silver, gold, and mithril.
properties.description_summary: Desolate moorland of northwestern Eriador, the combined result of Angmar's wars, the Great Plague and abandonment. Heather over old farmsteads, peat-brown rivers and wandering Dúnedain Rangers as the only witnesses.

properties.name: Eregion
properties.description_text: Eregion is a Noldorin-founded realm on the western side of the Misty Mountains, centered on the great city of Ost-in-Edhil and famed for its jewel-smiths, close friendship with Khazad-dûm, and the forging of the Rings of Power.
properties.description_summary: Former Noldorin realm at the western foot of the Misty Mountains, now a land of ruins and surviving holly trees. Ost-in-Edhil sacked, the West-door of Moria opening by moonlight on the Eithel Nargil lake; memory and loss in equal measure.

properties.name: Moria
properties.description_text: Moria, or Khazad-dûm, is the ancient and legendary underground Dwarf-city of the House of Durin, carved deep beneath the Misty Mountains. Now a vast, dark abyss occupied by Orcs, Cave-Trolls, and the shadow of the Balrog.
properties.description_summary: Ancient Dwarven city beneath three Misty Mountain peaks, now held by Orcs, Cave-trolls and the sleeping Balrog of the deep. Hundreds of miles of galleries, bottomless shafts and the unexplained rhythmic knockings in the lowest dark.

properties.name: Dunland
properties.description_text: Dunland is the rugged foothill and plateau country south of the Glanduin, east of the Gwathló and north of the Isen, a land of highlands and plains ruled by fractious Dunlending clans who herd, raid and scheme along the vital Old South Road and the Isen Gap.
properties.description_summary: Foothill and plateau country of the fractious Dunlending clans, people of long memory and bitter grudges against Rohirrim and Dúnedain alike. Heather hills, swift rivers and the Isen Gap as the vital artery of this lawless land.

properties.name: Forochel
properties.description_text: The Cape and Bay of Forochel are a rough, cold and storm-wracked peninsula and bay thrusting into Belegaer and the Encircling Sea, the last remnant of lands once lying beyond the Iron Mountains, scarred by Morgoth’s malice but rich in marine life, whaling, furs and treacherous ice. Geology consists of barren shelf of granite worn smooth by the passage of great ice sheets, with little accessible mineral wealth.
properties.description_summary: Arctic peninsula of bare glacier-smoothed granite where the Lossoth hunt seals on winter ice and summer icebergs drift in dark green waters. Austere tundra beauty of coloured mosses, alpine flowers and arctic seabirds.

properties.name: Mordor
properties.description_text: Mordor, the Black Land, is the volcanic plateau realm of the Dark Lord Sauron, walled in on three sides by the formidable mountain chains of Ephel Dúath and Ered Lithui. The plateau is arid, rugged, and heavily Orc-infested, serving as a fortress-kingdom of evil. Bedrock consists of black volcanic basalt giving the region its characteristic black, scorched appearance.
properties.description_summary: Sauron's volcanic plateau, walled in by Ephel Dûath and Ered Lithui. Orodruin erupts and coats the interior in black lava; the sky is perpetually grey. Two guarded gates and no exit for those who should not be there.

properties.name: Forithilien
properties.description_text: Forithilien, or North Ithilien, is the northern part of Gondor’s eastern Anduin province between the Great River and the Ephel Dúath, stretching from the Dead Marshes and Nindalf southward to the valley of the Ithilduin (later Morgulduin). It is the section of Ithilien most clearly shown in The Lord of the Rings, where Frodo, Sam and Gollum travel towards Cirith Ungol and where Faramir’s rangers maintain secret refuges like Henneth Annûn. In Gondor’s strength it is famed as a fair, fertile land of climbing woods, swift streams and rich gardens; by the late Third Age it is largely depopulated and serves as a hidden war-ground between Gondor’s rangers and the forces moving out of Mordor.
properties.description_summary: North Ithilien between the Anduin and the Ephel Dûath, a land of ancient gardens reclaimed by wild laurel, rosemary and jasmine over marble ruins. Gondorian guerrillas ambush Mordor's supply columns from the sweet-smelling undergrowth.

properties.name: Nurn
properties.description_text: Nurn is the more fertile, semi-arid southern province of Mordor surrounding the highly saline Lake Nurnen. Enriched by volcanic soils and watered by mountain streams, Nurn serves as the slave-worked breadbasket feeding the massive armies of Sauron.
properties.description_summary: The only productive farmland in Mordor, surrounding the saline Lake Nurnen. Slave-worked fields of sorghum and barley under Orc overseers; grey-green lake water, salt-white shores and a horizon that always smells of ash.

properties.name: Druwaith Laur
properties.description_text: Drúwaith Iaur, or Old Púkel-land, is an extremely remote, isolated, and obscure territory in northwestern Gondor, bordering Andrast to the west and the Westmarch to the east. Once claimed and nominally administered by Gondor, the region fell into extreme desertion and depopulation following the Great Plague of T.A. 1636. By T.A. 1640, no Dúnedain remain, leaving the wild, rugged, and uncultivated terrain to be ruled at a clan level by scattered tribes of Dunmen (Dunir) and native Drúedain (Wild Men).
properties.description_summary: Remote northwestern Gondor, abandoned by the Dúnedain after the Great Plague. Wild Drúedain and Dunir clans rule rocky hills and ravines of short violent waterfalls, invisible to and uncaring of any outside authority.

properties.name: Hairavercien
properties.description_text: Southern Gondor comprises the fertile river valleys, coastal plains, highland vales and mountain flanks between the White Mountains (Ered Nimrais) and the Bay of Belfalas, encompassing the provinces of Lebennin, Belfalas/Dor-en-Ernil, Lamedon, Anfalas and the Pinnath Gelin—the heartland of Númenórean civilization in Middle-earth.
properties.description_summary: Sub-region in the upper Morthond valleys, defined by its rock fords: easy summer crossings over bright water, dangerous winter traps of ice and flood. Pine and mountain birch, Gondorian miners and alpine herders in the high vales.

properties.name: Andrast
properties.description_text: Southern Gondor comprises the fertile river valleys, coastal plains, highland vales and mountain flanks between the White Mountains (Ered Nimrais) and the Bay of Belfalas, encompassing the provinces of Lebennin, Belfalas/Dor-en-Ernil, Lamedon, Anfalas and the Pinnath Gelin—the heartland of Númenórean civilization in Middle-earth.
properties.description_summary: Westernmost cape of Gondor where the White Mountains plunge into Belegaer in vertical cliffs. Dark fern-and-yew valleys shelter the Orodbedhrim tribe of Drúedain, so invisible in the terrain that Gondor's maps cannot find them.

properties.name: Rivendell
properties.description_text: The Vale of Imladris (Rivendell) is a deeply cloven, hidden valley at the western feet of the Misty Mountains in eastern Rhudaur, carved by the Bruinen (Loudwater). It forms a small but varied enclave of high hills, steep forested slopes and a sheltered inner valley dominated by Elrond’s house. Surrounded by the harsher lands of Rhudaur (Highlands, Trollshaws and Lowlands), Rivendell is an ‘island of refuge’ where Elves and Dúnedain hold back the darkness of Trolls, Hillmen, Wolves and Orcs in the wider region.[file:244]
properties.description_summary: Hidden valley in the feet of the Misty Mountains, carved by the Bruinen, with Elrond's house growing from the valley walls amid year-round gardens and waterfalls. An island of knowledge and refuge where the weary find relief.

properties.name: Lone-lands
properties.description_text: Rhudaur is the eastern successor state of Arnor, a rugged, sparsely settled land between the Ettenmoors, the Trollshaws and the Angle. It stretches from the Ettenmoors and Ettendales in the north down through the Pinefells and Trollshaws to the rolling lowlands between the rivers Mitheithel and Bruinen, forming a harsh frontier of jagged hills, evergreen forests, boggy vales and predator-haunted woodlands.
properties.description_summary: Broad heather plains between Bree and the Mitheithel, once part of prosperous Arnor, now almost empty. Ruined Dúnedain towers as the only landmarks and the Great East Road to follow; merchants carry their own provisions because nothing else is here.

properties.name: Fangorn
properties.description_text: Fangorn is an ancient forest on the southeastern slopes of the Misty Mountains, bordered to the north by the plains running to the Silverlode (Celebrant), to the west by the high peaks, to the east by the Wold and to the south by the open plains of Calenardhon. It is the last great remnant of the immense forest that once covered much of Eriador and Calenardhon and is uniquely tended by the Ents, who act as tree-herds and guardians of this 'giant garden'. The module also treats the surrounding borderlands along the Entwash and Limlight as part of the same regional system.
properties.description_summary: The oldest living forest in Middle-earth, tended by Ents who murmur in primordial tongues. Green filtered light, damp earth and bark, and time that flows differently inside its borders. The last remnant of the primeval woods of the world.

properties.name: Dor Guldur
properties.description_text: Southern Mirkwood and Southern Rhovanion form a wild, embattled region stretching from the Old Forest Road down to the Dagorlad and the Emyn Muil, dominated by the dark forest around Dol Guldur, the horse-plains of Talath Harroch, and the Anduin vales and Brown Lands that lie between Mirkwood and Gondor. Geology consists of primarily sedimentary strata of limestone and shale, with significant granite projections such as the cinder cone of amon lanc and the mountains of mirkwood.
properties.description_summary: Dol Guldur's fortress dominates southern Mirkwood from Amon Lanc. Trees twisted toward darkness, Great Spider webs blocking every path and the smell of decay that stays in the clothes for days. The Brown Lands stretch dead beyond the Anduin.

properties.name: Lorien
properties.description_text: Lórien, the Golden Wood, is a secluded Elven forest-realm east of the Misty Mountains, ruled by Galadriel and Celeborn and defined by its golden mallorn trees, timeless beauty, and potent yet subtle Ring-lore.
properties.description_summary: Galadriel's Golden Wood, where mallorn trees hold their golden leaves through winter and a light lingers from an earlier age. Time passes differently here; the Galadhrim watch from the treetops and the two rivers guard its borders.

properties.name: The Shire
properties.description_text:
The Shire was a country in northwestern Middle-earth, inhabited by the Hobbits. It was located in the region of Eriador, and was under the control of the Reunited Kingdom as a Free Land.
By the late Third Age it was one of the few heavily-populated areas left in Eriador. Its name in Westron was Sûza, "Shire," or Sûzat, "The Shire." Contrary to popular misconception, the Shire was not the birthplace of Frodo Baggins, as he was born in Buckland, which at the time was not formally part of the Shire despite being colonized by Shire hobbits.
properties.description_summary: The Hobbit homeland: ordered, fertile and deliberately apart from the outside world. Hedgerow-lined roads, smial-houses in the hillsides, good ale and well-kept gardens. Perhaps the happiest land in the Third Age.

properties.name: Bree
properties.description_text: Arthedain is the northern Dúnedain kingdom in Eriador, cradled between the Blue Mountains and the Misty Mountains and bounded by the rivers Lhûn and Baranduin, a cool, sparsely populated realm of rocky hills, downs, marshes, and the Shire, ruled from Fornost as the last Númenórean realm in the North in T.A. 1640.
properties.description_summary: Hill-town at the crossing of two ancient roads, the last real settlement before the empty lands of the north. Men and Hobbits sharing space without much fuss, and the Prancing Pony as the clearing-house for all rumours of the world.

properties.name: Dorwinion
properties.description_text: Dorwinion, or the 'Wineland,' is a wealthy and fertile country located along the northwest coast of the Sea of Rhûn and the lower reaches of the swift River Celduin and the rushing Uldona. Known throughout Middle-earth for producing the finest wines, its culture is dominated by the Dorwinrim, a hardy, adaptable, and highly diplomatic merchant people. Their capital, Shrel-Kain, is the largest port on the sea and serves as the mercantile and diplomatic hub of the entire region.
properties.description_summary: The Wineland on the northwest shore of the Sea of Rhûn, producer of the finest wines in Middle-earth. Shrel-Kain is the great inland sea's greatest port; the Dorwinrim are the diplomats and merchants who trade with half the world.
